new_classes.label_en: blended Scotch whisky in containers > 2 L (CN 2208 30 79)
new_classes.definition_en: Blended Scotch whisky (a spirit distilled from a mash of cereals, distilled and matured in Scotland, marketed at an alcoholic strength by volume of 40 % vol or higher) presented in containers of a nominal volume greater than 2 litres, without added aerated water.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the four main distinguishing criteria for CN 2208 30 79: (1) blended Scotch whisky type (via subclass of BlendedScotchWhisky), (2) Scottish origin/maturation (via ScotchWhiskyOriginQuality restriction), (3) container volume > 2 L (via LargeContainer part restriction), and (4) absence of added aerated/soda water (via complement restriction on SodaWater part). The minimum alcoholic strength of 40 % vol is also expressed via a decimalRange restriction on alcoholByVolumePercent. A small coverage gap remains because the legal text references a broader whisky definition (distilled from a mash of cereals) that is already inherited from parent classes and not re-stated here, and the blending process distinguishing this from single-malt/single-grain is captured at the parent BlendedScotchWhisky level rather than repeated. The complement restriction on SodaWater is an approximation — the exclusion of whisky-soda is expressed as 'not has part SodaWater', which correctly excludes the soda-containing sibling code 2208 30 61. Overall coverage is high but not complete due to the indirect modelling of the blending criterion and the cereal-mash distillation criterion at the parent level.
